title: Say Hello

contentBlocks.text: Every programmer's first trick is making the computer say something. In Python, you do that with print().
Whatever you put between the quotation marks inside print() shows up on the screen exactly as you typed it.
Don't forget the quotation marks around your message — Python needs them to know where your text starts and ends.
In the editor, write one line of code that prints exactly: Hello, World!

starterCode: # Use print() to make Python say something

testCases.successMessage: You did it — Python said hello!
testCases.failureMessage: We were expecting the output to be exactly: Hello, World!

hints: Start with the word print, then a set of parentheses: print()
Put your message inside quotation marks, between the parentheses: print("Hello, World!")
